item.title: The trick to powerful public speaking | Lawrence Bernstein
item.itunes:author: Lawrence Bernstein
item.description: Why do so many of us get nervous when public speaking? Communication expert Lawrence Bernstein says the key to dealing with the pressure is as simple as having a casual chat. He introduces the "coffee shop test" as a way to help you overcome nerves, connect with your audience and deliver a message that truly resonates.
item.itunes:subtitle: The trick to powerful public speaking | Lawrence Bernstein
item.itunes:summary: Why do so many of us get nervous when public speaking? Communication expert Lawrence Bernstein says the key to dealing with the pressure is as simple as having a casual chat. He introduces the "coffee shop test" as a way to help you overcome nerves, connect with your audience and deliver a message that truly resonates.
item.author: contact@ted.com (TED)

item.title: Our savory, spicy, significant relationship with food | June Jo Lee
item.itunes:author: June Jo Lee
item.description: Exploring the rich meaning behind what we choose to eat, food ethnographer June Jo Lee unpacks how our "modern hungers" — or our deepest desires and need for connection — inform every bite. She decodes what Gen Z's palate says about the present moment and the shape of the future.
item.itunes:subtitle: Our savory, spicy, significant relationship with food | June Jo Lee
item.itunes:summary: Exploring the rich meaning behind what we choose to eat, food ethnographer June Jo Lee unpacks how our "modern hungers" — or our deepest desires and need for connection — inform every bite. She decodes what Gen Z's palate says about the present moment and the shape of the future.
item.author: contact@ted.com (TED)

item.title: How community-led research drives social change | Monica Malta
item.itunes:author: Monica Malta
item.description: What's the best way to develop and implement solutions to social problems? TED Fellow and human rights activist Monica Malta discusses why traditional, top-down policymaking often fails by excluding key voices and missing root causes. She shows why community-based participatory research — a method that empowers communities to co-create solutions to their own challenges — taps into the resilience and strength of everyday people to be leaders of change.
item.itunes:subtitle: How community-led research drives social change | Monica Malta
item.itunes:summary: What's the best way to develop and implement solutions to social problems? TED Fellow and human rights activist Monica Malta discusses why traditional, top-down policymaking often fails by excluding key voices and missing root causes. She shows why community-based participatory research — a method that empowers communities to co-create solutions to their own challenges — taps into the resilience and strength of everyday people to be leaders of change.
item.author: contact@ted.com (TED)

item.title: How AI is saving billions of years of human research time | Max Jaderberg
item.itunes:author: Max Jaderberg
item.description: Can AI compress the yearslong research time of a PhD into seconds? Research scientist Max Jaderberg explores how “AI analogs” simulate real-world lab work with staggering speed and scale, unlocking new insights on protein folding and drug discovery. Drawing on his experience working on Isomorphic Labs' and Google DeepMind's AlphaFold 3 — an AI model for predicting the structure of molecules — Jaderberg explains how this new technology frees up researchers' time and resources to better understand the real, messy world and tackle the next frontiers of science, medicine and more.
item.itunes:subtitle: How AI is saving billions of years of human research time | Max Jaderberg

item.author: contact@ted.com (TED)

item.title: Can curiosity heal division? | Scott Shigeoka
item.itunes:author: Scott Shigeoka
item.description: Curiosity is about seeking what unites us rather than clinging to what divides us, says author and curiosity expert Scott Shigeoka. Drawing on his research into fostering connection and healing division, he shares what he learned traveling across the US meeting people with views opposing his own — and shows why recognizing our shared humanity begins with getting curious.
item.itunes:subtitle: Can curiosity heal division? | Scott Shigeoka

item.author: contact@ted.com (TED)

item.title: How I imitate nature's voices | Snow Raven
item.itunes:author: Snow Raven
item.description: You're about to hear the sounds of several different creatures — from the voice of one single musician. In a spellbinding talk and performance, singer Snow Raven mimics the hoot of an owl, the grumble of a bear, the howl of a wolf and more.
item.itunes:subtitle: How I imitate nature's voices | Snow Raven

item.author: contact@ted.com (TED)

item.title: This refrigerator is saving lives | Norah Magero
item.itunes:author: Norah Magero
item.description: TED Fellow and renewable energy expert Norah Magero envisions an Africa that pioneers its own technological future, shifting the narrative from dependence and consumption to self-reliance and innovation. She shares how she developed VacciBox — a solar-powered refrigeration system made in Kenya to keep vaccines cold in off-grid areas — and highlights the power of homegrown solutions to transform health care.
item.itunes:subtitle: This refrigerator is saving lives | Norah Magero

item.author: contact@ted.com (TED)

item.title: The greatest show on Earth — for kids who need it most | Sahba Aminikia
item.itunes:author: Sahba Aminikia
item.description: TED Fellow and composer Sahba Aminikia brings the healing power of dance, storytelling, music and performance to some of the most dangerous places on Earth. By celebrating children and their communities with beauty and joy, he shows how to cultivate hope, connection and love — even in conflict zones. "The ultimate power is in unity," Aminikia says.
item.itunes:subtitle: The greatest show on Earth — for kids who need it most | Sahba Aminikia

item.author: contact@ted.com (TED)

item.title: A hospital in the cloud bringing health care anywhere in the world | Mohamed Aburawi
item.itunes:author: Mohamed Aburawi
item.description: What if AI could help connect you with the right medical care, exactly when you need it? Health systems entrepreneur, surgeon and TED Fellow Mohamed Aburawi explores how his digital health platform, Speetar, uses AI to bridge the healthcare gap in underserved regions, like his native Libya, by connecting patients with doctors who truly understand their needs.
item.itunes:subtitle: A hospital in the cloud bringing health care anywhere in the world | Mohamed Aburawi

item.author: contact@ted.com (TED)

item.title: What does poverty look like on a plate? | Huiyi Lin
item.itunes:author: Huiyi Lin
item.description: TED Fellow and economic policy researcher Huiyi Lin is cocreator of "The Poverty Line," an art project examining poverty through the lens of food. By photographing the daily food choices of people living at the poverty line in 38 countries and territories around the world, Lin shines a light on the problem of poverty in a way no policy report ever could.
item.itunes:subtitle: What does poverty look like on a plate? | Huiyi Lin

item.author: contact@ted.com (TED)

item.title: Why do some bodies respond differently to disease? | Erika Moore
item.itunes:author: Erika Moore
item.description: TED Fellow and equity bioengineer Erika Moore investigates how cells controlling inflammation behave differently depending on a patient's background. By focusing on the "who" behind the disease, Moore is uncovering why certain diseases disproportionately affect certain ethnicities, paving the way for more inclusive and effective health care.
item.itunes:subtitle: Why do some bodies respond differently to disease? | Erika Moore
item.itunes:summary: TED Fellow and equity bioengineer Erika Moore investigates how cells controlling inflammation behave differently depending on a patient's background. item.author: contact@ted.com (TED)

item.title: Why smell matters more than you think | Paule Joseph
item.itunes:author: Paule Joseph
item.description: TED Fellow and chemosensory researcher Paule Joseph unveils the hidden power of a sense that's too often overlooked: smell. She delves into the science behind smell — from how it evokes memory and emotion to its potential for early disease detection — and advocates for the creation of a baseline test for taste and smell that could open the door to more comprehensive health care.
item.itunes:subtitle: Why smell matters more than you think | Paule Joseph

item.author: contact@ted.com (TED)

item.title: What you can learn crossing the ocean in a canoe | Lehua Kamalu
item.itunes:author: Lehua Kamalu
item.description: “Being on a voyage forces you to unplug,” says TED Fellow and third-generation captain Lehua Kamalu. Sharing ancient knowledge from traditional Hawaiian ocean voyaging, she reflects on the transformational power of seafaring journeys — and what it means to travel mindfully, no matter where you are.
